text.title: django-pytest Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for django-pytest. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Set DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE in pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] to a test-specific settings file with DEBUG=False, in-memory cache, fake email backend, and MD5PasswordHasher.
rule.rationale: Test settings reduce overhead and prevent accidental real email/payment sends. MD5PasswordHasher speeds up tests by 10-100x but must never leak into dev/prod settings.

rule.statement: Keep conftest.py close to where fixtures are used. Project-root conftest only for truly global fixtures (api_client, user, auth_client, network-blocking).
rule.rationale: Auto-discovery cascades — nested conftest fixtures shadow each other silently if not scoped.

rule.statement: Add an autouse network-blocking fixture in the root conftest that raises on any socket.create_connection call. Tests that need real network must explicitly opt out.
rule.rationale: Tests that forget to mock external services produce 30s timeouts, not clear failures. A loud guard makes the gap obvious immediately.

rule.statement: Use factory_boy DjangoModelFactory instead of fixture-as-function for test data. Override fields per test with UserFactory(email='x@y.z').
rule.rationale: factory_boy handles sequences and related objects; plain fixtures-as-functions require manual wiring for each variant.

rule.statement: Mark test classes or functions with @pytest.mark.django_db only when DB access is required. Pure logic tests must not touch the DB.
rule.rationale: DB setup costs time; pure logic tests run much faster without it. --strict-markers makes missing markers a hard error.

rule.statement: Structure each test: Arrange (set up data) → Act (call code under test) → Assert (verify observable outcome). One logical assertion group per test.
rule.rationale: Tests with multiple unrelated assertions fail for multiple reasons simultaneously, making the root cause ambiguous.

rule.statement: Use APIClient.force_authenticate instead of issuing real login requests in integration tests — much faster and tests the endpoint, not the auth flow.
rule.rationale: Real login calls include password hashing and token generation — 100-1000ms per test. force_authenticate bypasses all of that.

rule.statement: Use @pytest.mark.parametrize for boundary and equivalence-class testing where the same logic runs against multiple inputs.
rule.rationale: Replaces repetitive test functions with a single declaration; failures show exactly which parameter set failed.

rule.statement: Mock at the I/O boundary (HTTP, mail, payment), test the rest with real DB and real serialization. Over-mocking produces tests that prove nothing.
rule.rationale: Mocking internal methods tests implementation, not behavior. Tests that mock the wrong import path pass silently with zero real coverage.

rule.statement: Use transactional_db (not db) when the code under test calls transaction.on_commit — on_commit callbacks never fire inside a rolled-back transaction.
rule.rationale: db wraps each test in a savepoint that never commits; on_commit handlers that send emails or dispatch tasks are silently skipped.

rule.statement: Require agents to read tests/conftest.py before generating any test. Prompt: "Read tests/conftest.py first. Reuse existing fixtures."
rule.rationale: LLMs default to inline creation rather than reuse; reading the file first surfaces the available fixture names.

rule.statement: Require --strict-markers and a lint rule that finds DB-touching code in tests without @pytest.mark.django_db.
rule.rationale: LLMs omit the marker; the resulting error is clear but wastes tokens iterating. Catch it in lint, not at runtime.
